title: Perf: Rank action takes 2-4s — eliminate full vault scans from the write path
description: Confirmed in live testing 2026-07-02: clicking Rank takes ~2-4s. Causes: backlog_rank_task runs a full scan_noteplan_dir on every call (only to collect existing block IDs for collision checks), plus two sequential get_note MCP round-trips, plus the frontend reload() triggering another full vault scan via get_backlog. Fixes: cache/reuse the scanned NoteStore (or maintain an in-memory block-id set), batch the MCP calls where possible, and make the post-write UI update incremental instead of a full rescan. Was consciously deferred in review as 'non-hot-path' — it IS the hot path for the Backlog UX.
notes: RESOLVED-pending-merge (2026-07-03): rank total now 45-79ms (was 17-33s). Root cause was noteplan-mcp TITLE resolution (2-6s/call); filename addressing is 3-17ms/call. Round 1 (cache/suppression) + round 2 (7->4 calls, filename addressing, local cache patch) both on fix/rank-perf / PR #6. Bridge-backend guard being added, then merge.
close_reason: Merged PR #6: rank 33s -> ~50ms (filename addressing + 4 calls + cache + bridge guard)

title: Reconcile mcp/tools.rs with the REAL NotePlan MCP schema (blocks backlog writes)
description: MCP Inspector against @noteplanco/noteplan-mcp revealed tools.rs was written against an assumed schema. The Phase 2 backlog write path FAILS at runtime (fails safe — invalid action rejected, nothing written — but non-functional). Confirmed real schema: (1) noteplan_get_notes has NO 'action' param; needs includeContent:true; returns a JSON envelope with the body in .content (our get_note must PARSE .content, not return raw JSON); default limit 500 lines + hasMore (must request full note or paginate or abort-safe). Line base = 1, matches on-disk (resolves w3o line-base nit). (2) noteplan_edit_content actions are insert/append/delete_lines/edit_line/replace_lines (NOT replace/delete); content field is 'content' NOT 'text'. Confirmed edit_line{title,line,content} works and needs no confirmationToken. delete must be delete_lines{startLine,endLine}; insert needs position:'at-line'. (3) All edit tools return {success:bool,...} — we must parse success and return Err on failure (data-safety: detect failed writes). (4) Dead-but-wrong wrappers: manage_note move uses destinationFolder (not toFolder); ui action is open_note (not open); paragraphs search has no 'completed' (use status); eventkit action get_events + requires source. All destructive tools expose dryRun+confirmationToken (potential extra safety layer — TBD). Fix on feat/backlog-write-path before merging PR #4.
close_reason: tools.rs reconciled to real MCP schema; merged in PR #4

title: Calendar harvest shows every reschedule hop: render only the live task instance, not >date ghosts
description: User report 2026-07-06 (PR #10 smoke): rescheduling a task in NotePlan leaves a '>new-date' forwarded copy (TaskState::Scheduled) in each hop's daily note; NotePlan greys those out and shows only the final instance, but our calendar harvest includes Open|Scheduled so the Backlog pool shows the ENTIRE chain (screenshot: 5x 'Fix BBQ' across 2025-W52..2026-07-18, only the last real). Likely fix: harvest only TaskState::Open from calendar notes (the live instance at the final stop is Open with a <date rescheduled_from marker; every ghost is Scheduled). Decide whether project-folder pool keeps including Scheduled (current behavior — a >date task in a project note is scheduled-not-moved; probably keep). Add a fixture reschedule chain (daily A '>B', daily B '<A' open) to pin it. Related: akw item (3) raised the same rescheduled-task semantics for the Filing tab.
close_reason: Merged to main (PR #12-#16).

title: Tag-scoped contexts: #tag entries under ## headings in #np-projects pull matching calendar tasks into that context
description: User design direction 2026-07-06 (from Priorities IA smoke session, PR #10): calendar tasks currently join EVERY context's pool, which surfaces home chores under Work. Proposal (user's, refined): allow list items under a '## Context' heading in #np-projects that are bare hashtags (e.g. '#work') alongside project refs; a calendar task joins that context when its tags intersect the context's tags. DECIDED: (1) untagged calendar tasks keep showing under every context (follows NotePlan Dashboard plugin convention); (2) tags pull CALENDAR tasks only — vault-wide tagged-task pull was considered and rejected for friction (project membership stays the opt-in for folder tasks); (3) helper wanted: surface 'tagged tasks that are NOT calendar and NOT in a tracked project folder' (likely an analyzer finding or a small Backlog affordance) to prompt adding their projects to #np-projects. This is explicit opt-in config, distinct from the earlier-rejected 'infer context from content' heuristic. Touches parse_contexts (projects.rs — distinguish #tag items from project refs), build_backlog calendar gate (backlog.rs), spec update, fixture vault. Needs a short design pass (syntax + Board/Backlog interaction + whether tag-matched tasks also stop appearing in non-matching contexts) before implementation — next up after PR #10 merges.
close_reason: Implemented and merged in PR #11 (squash e82df13). Tag-scoped contexts: #tag declarations under ## headings in #np-projects scope calendar tasks; hierarchical tags; StrayTaggedTaskAnalyzer; context-tag caption.

title: Backlog remove-from-ranking fails: delete_lines now demands confirmationToken; dryRun token flow is a data-safety trap
description: User report 2026-07-04: clicking x on a ranked Backlog row toasts 'Remove failed: edit did not report success: Confirmation token is required for noteplan_delete_lines. Call noteplan_delete_lines with dryRun=true to get a new confirmationToken.' Our success-parse guard correctly aborted (nothing written blind) — the failure is that the server (current @noteplanco/noteplan-mcp) now ENFORCES confirmationToken for delete_lines, which the remove path (backlog_write.rs planner -> tools.rs delete_lines on the #np-backlog CONTROL note) never implemented. HAZARD before fixing: upstream dryRun is BROKEN (NotePlan/noteplan-mcp#8, verified 2026-07-02: writes execute despite dryRun=true), so the server's suggested 'call with dryRun=true to get a token' flow may PERFORM the delete during the 'preview'. Do NOT implement blind token-fetch. Fix plan: (1) re-verify current noteplan-mcp delete_lines/dryRun behavior in MCP Inspector (maybe upstream fixed #8; version-check), (2) if dryRun is still unsafe, redesign remove to avoid delete_lines entirely — e.g. edit_line the ranked entry to an empty/tombstone line (edit_line needs no token per 8zl findings) or rewrite the ranked block via edit_line ops, (3) whatever the mechanism, keep relocate-by-content verify-before-write and end with the human empirical gate. Target is the app-owned control note, not user content, so removal there is policy-permitted.
notes: 2026-07-06: PR #10 shipped Backlog/Board WITHOUT Unrank controls (deliberate, no dead buttons). This bead's fix unblocks: Unrank on both views (Phase 2 of the Priorities IA spec, docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-04-priorities-ia-redesign-design.md §7) plus Complete-from-Board (verify noteplan_paragraphs complete action in the same Inspector session). Interim user workaround: hand-edit the #np-backlog note in NotePlan.
close_reason: Merged to main (PR #12-#16).

title: Tasks search always returns nothing: search_tasks wrapper schema never verified against noteplan_paragraphs
description: User report 2026-07-03: Tasks view search (e.g. 'HELOC', a task that visibly exists in the Backlog) always shows 'No tasks found matching your search.' mcp/tools.rs:290 search_tasks calls noteplan_paragraphs with {action:'search', query, status:'open'|'done'} — and the doc comment (tools.rs:285-289) explicitly says the schema was NOT re-confirmed in MCP Inspector. Every other wrapper written against an assumed schema was wrong (see bead 8zl / docs/testing-with-mcp-inspector.md), so this one is presumed mismatched too: the server may reject the action/params or return an envelope TaskTriage's parser doesn't match, both rendering as zero results silently. Fix: verify the real noteplan_paragraphs search schema in MCP Inspector (human procedure per docs/testing-with-mcp-inspector.md), reconcile the wrapper args + response parsing in tools.rs and TaskTriage.tsx, and surface tool errors instead of an empty state. Also check complete_task (tools.rs:307) against the same schema while in there.
notes: 2026-07-04: i0g redesign may supersede this — the Tasks view is slated to become a scan-based all-tasks inventory (ranked vs unranked) instead of an MCP-search UI. Decide during the i0g brainstorm whether search gets fixed as a filter on the inventory or the noteplan_paragraphs wrapper work is dropped/deferred (complete_task verification still matters for task completion).
close_reason: Superseded by the Priorities IA redesign (PR #10): the Tasks view was retired; search is now a client-side filter over scanned tasks in the merged Backlog. The unverified noteplan_paragraphs search wrapper was deleted; complete_task remains for Phase 2 and still needs Inspector verification (tracked on ih2/Phase-2 work)

title: Backlog rows need task metadata: project/folder, priority, hashtags
description: User report 2026-07-03: ranked and pool rows in the Backlog show only truncated task text (+ a note ref like '01 - financier - New Thou…' on pool rows). Not enough to rank confidently — want per-row: source project and/or folder, ! priority, and any #hashtags. parse_task_line (parser/task.rs) already tokenizes priority/tags/dates, so the data likely exists at parse time; check whether RankedTask/PoolTask (types/api.ts:~120-137) carry it over IPC or need new fields (Rust model + TS type sync, no codegen). Render as small badges/secondary line per row in Backlog.tsx. Also consider showing note title on RANKED rows (currently pool-only).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 (squash 63a7795): Board = ranked work queue, merged Backlog with grouped inventory + calendar harvesting; r2t metadata absorbed into TaskCard; b68 Show-older reachability fixed via Copilot followup

title: Backlog drag-reorder inert: Tauri dragDropEnabled default swallows HTML5 DnD in WKWebView
description: User report 2026-07-03 (post PR #8 smoke): dragging a ranked Backlog row starts (grab cursor, drag image) but dropping never reorders. Backlog.tsx wires draggable/onDragStart/onDragOver/onDrop -> backlogReorder correctly, but tauri.conf.json sets no dragDropEnabled on the window and Tauri v2 defaults it to TRUE, so the native (wry) drag-drop handler intercepts drop/dragover inside the WKWebView and the HTML5 events never reach the frontend. The Phase 2 empirical gate exercised the pool Rank BUTTON (click path), never drag-reorder, so this was never caught. Fix: add "dragDropEnabled": false to the window config in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json (app has no native file-drop features — grep confirms), then HUMAN empirical gate: drag a ranked row in the real app and verify the control note reorders on disk via MCP. Data-safety relevant (write path) — needs its own verification run, not a ride-along.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #9; human empirical gate passed (drag-reorder writes control note correctly; Board disclosure survives view switches)

title: Filing tab UX: explain purpose, suppress template/boilerplate blocks, rethink rescheduled-task blocks
description: User feedback 2026-07-03 on first working use of the Filing tab (it was IPC-broken until PR #7, possibly never functional in a shipped build): (1) No purpose affordance — nothing explains the tab breaks a daily note into blocks and suggests project/domain notes to file them into, or what File does (appends via MCP). Add a header blurb/empty-state explainer. (2) Template noise — every ## section is offered as a fileable block, so daily-template boilerplate (Journal/Todo/Agenda, incl. empty '## Todo\n*') shows as candidates with 'no matching targets'. Detect/suppress template-originated + empty sections (compare against @Templates content? skip no-target-no-content blocks?). (3) Rescheduled-task blocks — tasks NotePlan carried forward (<date syntax) render as a filing block; semantics differ from new content (already tracked elsewhere; maybe belong under Priorities/Backlog instead). Child of the UX overhaul epic.

title: Build committed fixture vault for headless read-path dev + regression tests
description: Per tooling decision 2026-07-02: create a tests/fixture-vault/ (or similar) directory modeling a realistic NotePlan data dir: Notes/ with JD area/category folders (work + personal areas), _NotePlan Organizer/ with #np-projects and #np-backlog control notes, Calendar/ dailies, tasks covering every syntax edge (!/!!/!!! priorities, ^blockIds, >dates, <dates, [x]/[-]/[>] states, tags, mentions, bare - list items, + leaders), @Templates/@Archive/@Trash/_attachments exclusion cases, >1000-line note for the hasMore guard, duplicate titles, unresolved control-note refs. Wire integration tests that run scan_noteplan_dir + build_project_board + build_backlog against it. This becomes the standard harness for all future read-path work.
close_reason: Fixture vault + integration harness merged in PR #5

title: Epic: UI/UX overhaul — unify navigation, MCP connect flow, rebrand-era design pass
description: User feedback 2026-07-02 after Phase 2 landed: the stacked toggle rows (Findings|Assessment|Priorities|Filing|Tasks, then Board|Backlog, then Work|Home) are 'super funky'; the MCP connect UX (tiny status-tray toggle) is wonky/undiscoverable. Now that the app has outgrown its original findings-list identity (analysis + filing + task management), do a whole-app UX rethink: navigation structure (maybe sidebar), MCP connection flow (auto-connect? prominent state?), visual hierarchy. Coordinate with the rename (NotePlan Companion). Big effort — do a proper brainstorm/design phase first.
notes: Shell scope SHIPPED in PR #8 (branch worktree-ux-overhaul-shell, 13 commits): grouped sidebar (Plan/Organize/Health), gate-free launch (auto-scan + MCP auto-connect), quiet connection UX, header removed, rename to NotePlan Companion. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-ux-overhaul-shell-design.md; plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-03-ux-overhaul-shell.md. Post-merge: close ziy, then remaining children: akw (Filing content UX), yjs (scan-failure surfacing per spec §5). Bead 486 backend scoping still open (UI half landed in PR #8).

title: Rename app: NotePlan Organizer -> NotePlan Companion
description: User decision 2026-07-02: 'Organizer' fit when the app only policed the organization system; with task management (Priorities/Backlog) it's now a general companion. Rename touches: tauri.conf.json (productName, identifier, window title), package.json/Cargo.toml names, README, CLAUDE.md, header UI in App.tsx, repo name (turboladen/noteplan-organizer -> ?; GitHub redirects old URLs), beads prefix (bd rename-prefix — big, maybe skip), icons unchanged. Coordinate with the UX overhaul epic so branding lands once.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #8: productName/window title/package names/README renamed to NotePlan Companion; identifier + app_lib + repo name deliberately unchanged

title: Audit pre-existing Tauri commands for camelCase IPC arg mismatch
description: Found while testing the backlog write path: Tauri v2 exposes command args to JS as camelCase by default, but src/api/commands.ts sends snake_case keys throughout, and NO pre-existing command uses rename_all. The new backlog commands are fixed (rename_all=snake_case, commit 03d4e14 on feat/backlog-write-path), but these pre-existing commands with multi-word args are likely broken on main today: get_content_blocks{note_path}, get_filing_suggestions{base_path,note_path}, export_assessment_context{guide_title}, mcp_call_tool{tool_name} — i.e. the Filing assistant tab and the FixAction 'Fix' button path may fail with 'missing required key'. Verify each in the running app, then annotate with #[tauri::command(rename_all="snake_case")] (matching the TS convention) and add a regression check. Single-word args (path, url, line) are unaffected.
close_reason: Merged PR #7: 4 broken commands fixed (Filing tab, Fix buttons, Export Context restored)

title: Board/Backlog: load from predefined project folder on demand, no full-vault scan or Scan-button gate
description:
Two compounding inefficiencies keep the Board/Backlog tab from being usable until the user clicks "Scan Notes":

1. Frontend gate: in App.tsx, the `activeTab === "priorities"` block that renders <ProjectBoard> only exists inside the `{report && (...)}` wrapper (around line 575). `report` is only set by `handleScan()`, which invokes the full `scan` Tauri command. So opening the Board tab first requires running the entire structural-analysis scan (all 8 analyzers via run_all_analyzers), even though the Board doesn't use `report.findings` at all.

2. Backend full-vault walk: independent of the UI gate, `get_project_board` (commands.rs) itself calls `scan_noteplan_dir(&path)`, which recursively parses every note in the whole NotePlan data directory. This is only to (a) find the `#np-projects` control note by tag search across all Regular notes (parser/projects.rs parse_project_control), and (b) resolve the project references it lists to their target notes/folders (resolve_folder). Neither step needs the rest of the vault (daily notes, unrelated JD categories, etc.) — the board should be able to open by reading just the control note plus the specific JD-category project folders it references.

Goal: the Board/Backlog view should populate on tab open (or app load) without requiring "Scan Notes" first, and the read path should be scoped to the known project-note location(s) rather than a full recursive scan_noteplan_dir call.

Open design question (no existing convention for this yet — checked config.rs, nothing there): how do we scope the read? Options to weigh: (a) require the #np-projects control note to live at a known top-level path so we can read just that file directly instead of tag-scanning all notes, then only recurse into the referenced JD-category folders; (b) keep tag discovery but cache/limit it to top-level notes; (c) something else. Needs a short design pass before implementation — this is a change to the read path used by both Phase 1 (read-only board, already shipped) and Phase 2 (backlog writes, in progress in ays).

Touches: src-tauri/src/parser/projects.rs (parse_project_control, build_project_board, resolve_folder), src-tauri/src/parser/mod.rs (scan_noteplan_dir), src-tauri/src/commands.rs (get_project_board), src/App.tsx (report-gated rendering of ProjectBoard/activeTab === "priorities").

Coordinate with ays (Phase 2 backlog write path, in progress) before starting — likely touches overlapping parser/projects.rs code.
notes: UI half DONE in PR #8: App.tsx no longer gates any view on the scan report; Board/Backlog/Filing take basePath from detected notePlanPath and populate on view entry. Remaining scope: backend — get_project_board/get_backlog still call scan_noteplan_dir (full-vault walk) internally; scope the read to the control note + referenced folders per this bead's design question.
close_reason: Merged to main (PR #12-#16).

title: Consolidate NotePlan line parsing into one shared tokenizer
description: FIRST task of Phase 2 (do before backlog.rs/backlog_write.rs are written). NotePlan task/line syntax is currently parsed in multiple places with subtly different rules (task.rs vs block.rs already diverge; Phase 2 would add copies in backlog.rs + backlog_write.rs planners). This is a data-safety risk: if a line is tokenized one way for display and another for verify-before-write, that mismatch is exactly what causes wrong-line writes. Extract a single hand-written parse_task_line(raw) -> ParsedTaskLine { leader, state, display_text, priority, block_id, tags, mentions, scheduled_to, rescheduled_from } and have task.rs, block.rs, backlog.rs, and the write planners all call it. NOT a Markdown AST lib (pulldown-cmark/comrak) — those normalize away the exact source offsets/line fidelity the line-addressed MCP write path depends on. No new dependency. Decision recorded 2026-07-01 (chose consolidate/hand-written over combinator lib or deferral). Then revise Phase 2 plan so backlog.rs/backlog_write.rs consume the shared tokenizer.
close_reason: Parser consolidation merged in PR #3

title: Add dprint catch-all + Prettier (React) + a format CI gate (none exists yet)
description:
Standardize formatting on the cross-repo model — kammerz is the reference: native tools own their domains, dprint is the catch-all for everything else. noteplan-organizer is the least-set-up of the four.

## Current state
- React 19 + Tauri + Vite (bun). Rust lives in `src-tauri/`.
- **Only eslint** (`bun run lint`) — no formatter at all: no dprint, no prettier.
- **No GitHub Actions CI at all** (no `.github/` directory). Has a `Justfile`.

## Target (match kammerz exactly)
- Add `dprint.jsonc` catch-all: `json` + `markdown` + `toml` + `pretty_yaml` plugins; exclude `node_modules`, `dist`, `src-tauri/target`, `.beads`, `.claude`. Pin plugin versions (align with kammerz).
- Add a native React/TS formatter — **Prettier** (matches kammerz; Biome is an acceptable alt). eslint stays for linting only.
- Rust (`src-tauri/`) → `cargo fmt`.
- Wire `just fmt` / `just fmt-check` in the existing Justfile to run all three (dprint + prettier + cargo fmt).
- **CI: none exists** — "update CI" here means *add* a minimal `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with at least a format gate (`just fmt-check`). Broader lint/test/build CI can be a follow-up bead.

Reference: kammerz `dprint.jsonc`, `frontend/.prettierrc`, `justfile` fmt/fmt-check targets, and CI `format` job.
acceptance_criteria: 'just fmt-check' runs dprint (md/json/toml/yaml) + prettier (React/TS) + cargo fmt --check clean; a new .github/workflows/ci.yml format job enforces it on PRs.

title: Phase 2: Backlog + drag-to-reorder write path
description: Block-ID round-trip validation spike (gate); #np-backlog reader (ranked+pool); pure verify-before-write planner (WriteOp enum makes content-note deletes unrepresentable); safety-gated MCP executor; backlog rank/reorder/remove commands; Backlog.tsx drag-to-reorder + Board/Backlog toggle. First feature that writes to NotePlan. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-priority-board-phase2-backlog-writes.md
close_reason: Backlog write path merged in PR #4

title: Phase 1: parsing foundation + read-only Board
description: Parse native ! priority + ^blockId on tasks; #np-projects reader; folder resolution + rollup; _NotePlan Organizer exclusion; get_project_board command; ProjectBoard.tsx + Priorities tab. Zero writes. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-priority-board-phase1-read-only-board.md
close_reason: Phase 1 read-only Board merged in PR #2

title: Epic: Project & Task Priority Board
description: Read-only project Board (rolls up tasks per JD-category project, ranked via #np-projects control note, sliced by context) + drag-to-reorder Backlog (per-context manual task ranking anchored to native NotePlan ^blockIds, written back via MCP under strict data-safety invariants). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-project-priority-board-design.md. Plans: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-priority-board-phase{1,2}-*.md

title: Exclude base 'Memo' folder (Memo AI sync) from analysis
description:
A base 'Memo' folder is now used to sync tasks into NotePlan from the 'Memo AI' app. These notes are not part of the JD organization hierarchy and should not be scanned/assessed — otherwise ID Consistency (and other analyzers) will flag them for missing/invalid IDs, missing hubs, being unfiled, etc.

Currently the folder-exclusion check ('@Archive', '@Trash', '@Templates', '_attachments') is duplicated inline via relative_path.contains(...) across ~12 analyzers plus parser/filing.rs and parser/hierarchy.rs. 'Memo' is not excluded anywhere.
design: The exclusion predicate is copy-pasted in ~14 sites. Prefer adding 'Memo' to a single shared helper (e.g. an is_excluded_path(relative_path) fn) and having analyzers call it, rather than pasting a 15th contains() check into each file. If a shared helper doesn't exist yet, introducing one is in scope and reduces future drift. Match on a leading path segment (e.g. starts_with("Memo/") / equals "Memo") rather than contains("Memo") to avoid false exclusions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Notes under a top-level 'Memo' folder are excluded from ID Consistency and all other analyzers, matching how @Archive/@Trash/_attachments are skipped
- Exclusion matches the base 'Memo' folder specifically (avoid over-matching notes that merely contain 'memo' in a path segment/title)
- Existing exclusions unchanged; add/adjust a test covering a Memo note being skipped
- cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml passes
notes: Sites using the current exclusion: analyzer/{id_consistency,stale_area,cross_wired_id,missing_hub,duplicates,broken_links,template_placeholders,hub_completeness,orphaned_notes,empty_structure,naming_inconsistency,unfiled}.rs and parser/{filing,hierarchy}.rs. Broader than ID Consistency but surfaced by the same org-scheme change.

title: ID Consistency: flag single-dot old-style hierarchical note IDs (incl. [Parent].00 old hubs)
description:
Org scheme changed: hubs are now '00.{PH,DH,RH} - Title' (HubCode) and notes use bare sequential IDs or ISO8601 dates (mixing is fine). The previous hub form '[Parent].00 - Title' (e.g. 10.00 - Hub) and old child IDs '[Parent].NN' (e.g. 10.02) are now unacceptable and should be flagged for migration.

The parser already classifies HubCode / DatePrefix / Sequential / BareHub correctly. The gap is in analyzer/id_consistency.rs: the JdDotted branch only flags note_id where dot_count >= 2 (three-segment like 10.01.01) AND note_id.starts_with(parent_prefix). Single-dot legacy IDs — the most common old form — are silently accepted:
  - '10.00 - Hub'  -> JdDotted, dot_count=1 -> NOT flagged (old hub, should become 00.PH/DH/RH)
  - '10.02 - Foo'  -> JdDotted, dot_count=1 -> NOT flagged (should become '02' or a date)

Also the parent_prefix gate means a dotted ID whose parent folder has no JD id (parent_jd_id=None) is never flagged, even though any dotted note ID is old-style under the new scheme.
design: Old hierarchical style is unacceptable across the board now, so flag ALL JdDotted note IDs rather than thresholding on depth. Special-case the '.00' suffix to point at the hub-code form since those are old hubs. Recommend bumping severity from Info to Warning for old hubs (bare 00 is already Error; hubs are structurally important); plain dotted children can stay Info or Warning — implementer's call, note it in the PR.
acceptance_criteria:
- Any Regular-note title ID classified as JdDotted is flagged (dot_count >= 1), not just >= 2
- Drop/relax the note_id.starts_with(parent_prefix) gate so dotted IDs are flagged regardless of parent folder id
- '[Parent].00' (ends in .00) suggests migrating to '00.PH' / '00.DH' / '00.RH'
- Other dotted IDs suggest migrating to a bare sequential id or ISO8601 date
- HubCode (00.PH/DH/RH), DatePrefix, and 2+ digit Sequential remain unflagged; bare '00' stays Error
- Unit tests added: single-dot old hub (10.00) flagged with hub-code suggestion; single-dot child (10.02) flagged with sequential/date suggestion; existing 3-segment and stale-filename tests still pass
- cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml passes
notes: Relevant files: src-tauri/src/analyzer/id_consistency.rs (lines ~56-87 JdDotted branch), src-tauri/src/parser/folder.rs::parse_note_id, parent_jd_id_from_path. References Hub (00.RH in '5x - References') already works via HubCode — no change needed for the References section.

title: Filing suggestion UI
description: Frontend component showing daily note blocks with suggested filing destinations. User can accept (triggers MCP noteplan_edit_content to move block) or dismiss.

description: Use folder structure + note titles to build a map of filing targets — existing project/domain/reference notes where blocks could be filed.

title: Daily note content block extraction
description: Parse daily notes into discrete content blocks (paragraphs, task groups, headings). Each block is a candidate for filing into a project/domain note.

title: Add config flag for parsing backend
description: Settings UI toggle: Rust (fast, file-based) vs MCP (NotePlan API). Only if benchmarks show MCP is viable. Depends on MCP integration and benchmark beads.
notes: Benchmark results (2026-03-16): Rust full parse ~1200ms for ~1600 notes. MCP list-only ~230ms, MCP get ~1ms/note (~1.6s total). MCP retrieval is competitive on raw I/O but doesn't include structural parsing — Rust parser still wins for bulk scanning. Config toggle is low-value: keep Rust for scanning, MCP for targeted ops (writes, search, embeddings). Recommend deferring or closing as won't-do.
close_reason: Benchmark shows Rust parser wins for bulk scanning. MCP get is fast (~1ms/note) but still needs Rust parsing on top. Config toggle adds complexity for no user benefit.

title: Benchmark Rust file parsing vs MCP note retrieval
description: Compare scan times for ~1600 notes using current Rust parser vs MCP noteplan_get_notes. Measure latency, throughput, and memory. Results inform whether a config toggle is worth adding.
close_reason: Benchmark command compares Rust scan_noteplan_dir vs MCP list + sample get. Results shown via toast. Config toggle (bead 3iz) decision deferred to after real-world benchmarking.

title: MCP server integration layer
description: Add Rust MCP client that can spawn and communicate with @noteplanco/noteplan-mcp via JSON-RPC/stdio. Optional dependency — app works without it.
close_reason: MCP integration layer implemented: rmcp client, typed tool wrappers, Tauri commands, frontend API + UI toggle

title: Add version display to app (crate version + git short rev)
description: Display app version in the traditional macOS 'About' spot. Show both the Cargo crate version and git short SHA. Investigate Tauri v2 conventions for version display.
close_reason: Implemented: build.rs embeds git short rev, get_git_rev Tauri command, core:app:default capability, frontend fetch via getVersion()+getGitRev(), status tray display with v0.1.0 (rev) format

title: Rework ID Consistency Analyzer
description: Remove parent-prefix matching, add duplicate sequential ID check, bare 00 error, and JD-dotted migration flag
close_reason: Closed

title: Restrict Cross-wired ID Analyzer to folders only
description: Skip notes with Sequential/DatePrefix/HubCode IDs, only check folder-to-folder hierarchy
close_reason: Closed

title: Add hub code detection to Missing Hub Analyzer
description: Detect hubs by 00.{P,D,R}H filename in addition to section-heading heuristic
close_reason: Closed

title: Add NoteIdKind enum to model layer
description: Add NoteIdKind enum (JdDotted, HubCode, Sequential, DatePrefix) to note.rs, add note_id_kind field to Note, and matching TS type
close_reason: Closed

title: Add hub code detection to Hub Completeness Analyzer
description: Same hub detection change as missing hub task
close_reason: Closed

title: Expand parser to recognize new ID formats
description: Add parse_note_id() to folder.rs with hub/date/sequential regexes, wire into parse_note()
close_reason: Closed

title: Update ID system to support sequential/date-based note IDs
description: Parent epic: Decouple note IDs from folder IDs. Notes use independent sequential integers, dates, or hub codes instead of requiring parent-prefix matching.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extend mock-MCP verify-before-write coverage to backlog_reorder + backlog_remove; DRY the single-note write protocol
description: Follow-up from w3o (2026-07-07), anticipated by the plan review. w3o added the Writer enum + MockMcp seam and extracted rank_task_inner with 6 mock tests (verify-before-write ordering, patch-on-success, round-2b partial-failure). backlog_reorder and backlog_remove were refactored to route through the new apply_ops(&Writer::Real) but were NOT given a testable *_inner seam, so their fetch→plan→apply→patch orchestration has ZERO mock-MCP coverage (only planner-level plan_reorder/plan_remove unit tests). CLAUDE.md literally mandates mock-MCP verify-before-write tests for write paths. Highest value: backlog_remove's tombstone is a ReplaceBacklogLine line-overwrite (the exact class the verify-before-write rule targets). Also: the single-note write protocol (Writer::Real construct + resolve + fetch_note_resilient + apply_ops + suppress + patch-on-success + timing log) now lives in 3 copies (rank_task_inner, reorder, remove) — extract a shared run_backlog_write helper to kill drift risk on the exact path where drift causes wrong-line writes, and add reorder_inner/remove_inner mock tests (verify-before-write ordering + patch-only-on-success). Harness already exists; this is incremental.

title: Wire Unrank UI: ✕ button on Board/Backlog calling backlogRemove (currently dead code)
description: Follow-up from ih2 (2026-07-07). ih2 fixes the remove-from-ranking MECHANISM (tombstone via edit_line), but src/api/commands.ts backlogRemove has ZERO component callers — no ✕ button shipped (PR #10 deliberately omitted Unrank controls to avoid dead buttons). Once ih2's human MCP Inspector + empirical gate pass, wire the ✕/Unrank affordance on Board.tsx and Backlog.tsx ranked rows to call backlogRemove, with the reconnect/offline handling the other write views use. Priorities IA spec §7. Also consider Complete-from-Board (verify noteplan_paragraphs complete action in the same Inspector session). BLOCKED on ih2 landing + gate.

title: Ranked path surfaces calendar reschedule ghosts (vgv only fixed the pool, not block_id-resolved ranked entries)
description: Follow-up from vgv code-review (2026-07-07). vgv dropped [>] reschedule ghosts from the calendar POOL, but the RANKED harvest resolves entries via block_id_index with NO state filter (deliberate per the vgv plan: ranked entries are user-curated, silently dropping one that went [>] would be worse). Consequence/tension: a task the user ranked, then rescheduled in NotePlan (leaving * [>] ... ^id greyed), still shows in the ranked list as resolved:true — the greyed ghost surfaces as an active ranked entry, contradicting vgv's 'render only the live instance' intent for the ranked case. Decide the right UX: mark such ranked entries as stale/ghost (state-aware), re-point to the live instance by matching cleaned text, or leave as-is. Related to w3o (ranked micro-window/block_id residuals).

title: Calendar harvest: standalone forward-scheduled [>] task whose Open copy isn't scanned is dropped from the pool
description: Follow-up from vgv code-review (2026-07-07). vgv's fix drops TaskState::Scheduled ([>]) tasks from ALL calendar-kind notes in build_backlog's pool, on the model that every [>] is a NotePlan move-ghost with a materialized Open live copy elsewhere. EDGE CASE: if the Open copy isn't in the scan — target daily beyond the 30-day window, target note not yet created, or a single-hop forward-schedule where the [>] is the only representation — the task disappears from the backlog pool entirely (note on disk untouched; visibility-only). Also applies to weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly notes which lack the dailies' 30-day-window natural pruner. NEEDS EMPIRICAL VERIFICATION against the live NotePlan vault: does NotePlan always materialize an Open copy when a task is forward-scheduled, or can [>] stand alone? If standalone [>] is real+common, distinguish move-ghosts (drop) from standalone forward-schedules (keep) rather than dropping all calendar Scheduled. VALIDATE during Board/Backlog empirical testing: confirm forward-scheduled tasks still appear in the backlog.

title: Guard Backlog post-write reloads (reorder/rank) against vault-switch race
description: Follow-up from mu1 plan review (2026-07-07). Backlog.tsx's imperative post-write reloads — commitReorder success (Backlog.tsx:79), rollback (:82), handleRank (:116) — stay UNGUARDED after mu1 adds the effect-scoped cancelled guard. A write in vault A then a basePath switch to B before the reload resolves would setData(A) while on B. Pre-existing (exists identically today), narrower than the mount/switch race mu1 fixes. Needs a generation-counter/ref (not an effect-local cancelled flag) since these calls aren't effect-scoped. Deferred from mu1 as a real complexity increment beyond 'final-review minors'.
close_reason: Merged to main (PR #12-#16).

title: Priorities IA redesign: Board = ranked work queue; Tasks = all-tasks inventory (planned vs backlog)
description: User design direction 2026-07-04 (supersedes the original a/b/c framing): the working paradigm is prioritize/rank/inspect/correct in the BACKLOG, then execute off the BOARD. Rank = 'ready to work on'. Therefore: (1) BOARD should show ONLY backlog-ranked tasks, in rank order (the work queue) — today it ranks projects and lists all their tasks, which reads as 'all tasks' and confuses the model; (2) TASKS view gets repurposed as the full inventory — all tasks, ranked + unranked, i.e. planned vs backlog (sprint-vs-backlog analogy) — replacing its current broken MCP-search implementation (see tqc: this direction may supersede fixing search as-is, or search becomes a filter on the inventory); (3) Backlog stays the grooming surface (coordinate with r2t row metadata). Needs a brainstorm/design pass before implementation — reshapes three views and the Board's data source (backlog contexts rather than #np-projects alone). Sprint/capacity planning is a separate future bead (see linked).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 (squash 63a7795): Board = ranked work queue, merged Backlog with grouped inventory + calendar harvesting; r2t metadata absorbed into TaskCard; b68 Show-older reachability fixed via Copilot followup

title: Board: persist per-project expansion state across view switches
description: User report 2026-07-03: expanding a project's task list on the Board, navigating to another sidebar view, and returning collapses everything — ProjectBoard unmounts on view switch so its local expanded useState (ProjectBoard.tsx:12) is lost. (Context-tab switches within the view keep state; only leaving the view resets.) Fix: persist the expanded-set — simplest is lifting to App.tsx alongside other per-view state, or a module-level cache/localStorage keyed by basePath+context; note ProjectBoard deliberately resets expansion when basePath changes (comment at :26) — preserve that behavior.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #9; human empirical gate passed (drag-reorder writes control note correctly; Board disclosure survives view switches)

title: Scan-failure surfacing per spec §5: Health views + footer indicator instead of global banner
description: Accepted deviation in the shell UX overhaul (PR #8): spec 2026-07-03-ux-overhaul-shell-design.md §5 says scan failures surface 'in Health views + footer indicator; rest of app unaffected', but the shipped shell reuses the global error banner in <main> (renders on every view) with no footer indicator, and the Health skeleton says 'Waiting for the first scan' without acknowledging failure. Final whole-branch review flagged it; accepted to keep the fix wave tight. Fix: route scan errors to the Findings/Assessment views + a sidebar-footer indicator; keep the global banner for dump/export/watch errors. Also fold in two ride-along minors from review: timeAgo() in Sidebar.tsx has no interval refresh (stale 'Nm ago' label), and no success toast on manual reconnect.

title: Scope watcher suppression to app-written paths (or trailing rescan on window close)
description: Fast follow-up from the rank-perf review (fix/rank-perf). The 4s watcher-suppression window is GLOBAL: any FS event during it is dropped, so a user edit to an UNRELATED note during the window isn't re-analyzed until the next FS event or manual rescan. Display/analysis staleness only — never corruption (the app never writes from watcher-derived state; the cache refresh patches the touched notes). Fix options: (a) scope suppression to the specific paths apply_ops wrote (needs title->path resolution), or (b) simpler: fire one trailing debounced rescan when the suppression window closes. Reviewer recommended (b)-style follow-up as sufficient.

title: Backlog rank: harden micro-window residuals (stale entry + get_note line-base abort)
description: Phase 2 backlog follow-ups from the write-path review (non-corrupting, deferred). (1) STALE ENTRY RACE: if the target task gets stamped with a different id in the sub-second window between the command's planning snapshot and the executor's fresh get_note, the executor correctly skips the double content-write (idempotent), but the backlog InsertBacklogLine — built at planning time with the planning-era block_id — still inserts an entry referencing the now-orphan id -> a stale/unresolved backlog entry on next read. App-owned note only, never content destruction, recoverable (reader shows it as stale; user removes/re-ranks). Fix would couple the stamp+insert ops through the executor so the backlog entry uses the id actually on the line. (2) get_note LINE-BASE NIT: plan_stamp_block_id still does a snapshot-line check against the frontend's on-disk line; if MCP get_note's line base differs from the on-disk scan it can spuriously abort a valid rank (user retries). Robustness, not safety — the authoritative gate is now content relocation (locate_unique_task_line). Consider dropping/relaxing the snapshot-line pre-check now that relocate-by-content is authoritative.
notes: Round-2 additions (2026-07-03, both non-corrupting fast-follows from the single-fetch review): (a) write-time idempotency re-check dropped — a concurrent stamp of the exact same line within the one-round-trip window gets its ^id overwritten (task text fully preserved; old backlog entry goes stale). (b) partial-failure collision-miss — cache patches only on full write success, so a stamp that landed before a later op failed isn't in the collision set until rescan; an immediate next rank could emit a duplicate id (read-side mis-resolution only). Options: on failure drop affected cache entries or trigger a scan. Original item 1 (stale-entry race) unchanged.

title: resolve_folder: bare non-JD refs can match nested subfolder by order
description: Phase 1 follow-up (flagged in review). parser/projects.rs resolve_folder() iterates notes and returns the first folder whose final segment matches the ref name (case-insensitive) or leading JD id. For a bare, non-JD reference (no numeric prefix) that collides with a nested subfolder name, resolution is order-dependent and may pick the wrong folder. Not triggered by the documented usage (JD-prefixed category refs like '32 - Product Ownership'). Fix options: prefer shallowest/topmost matching folder, or require JD-id match for disambiguation, or match against known JD-category folders only. Low priority.
close_reason: Merged to main (PR #12-#16).

title: Global task triage (Phase 4)
description: Use noteplan_paragraphs.search_global for structured task queries. Depends on MCP integration layer.

title: Semantic link suggestions (Phase 3)
description: Use noteplan_embeddings to find conceptually related but unlinked notes. Depends on MCP integration layer.

title: Fix actions on findings via MCP
description: Add Fix buttons to existing findings that can auto-remediate through MCP (move cross-wired notes, complete stale tasks, etc.). Depends on MCP integration layer.
close_reason: Fix actions: added FixAction model (label, tool, arguments) to Finding struct. StaleTask findings get 'Complete task' fix action via noteplan_paragraphs. Frontend shows hover-reveal Fix button on cards with fix_action, MCP-gated. Auto-dismisses on success.

title: Backlog rank: write-time idempotency re-check for the sub-ms in-memory window (round-2a, declined in w3o)
description: Deferred from w3o (2026-07-07). The single-fetch rank model plans in-memory between the source get_note and the stamp write with NO MCP round-trip, so the collision window is sub-millisecond CPU time. A concurrent same-user stamp of the EXACT same line within that window would get its ^id overwritten (task text fully preserved; the other actor's app-owned backlog entry goes stale — read-side only, non-corrupting). Closing this fully requires either re-fetching the source at write time (the 2-6s round-trip the single-fetch model deliberately dropped) or a backend compare-and-swap the MCP does not expose. Declined in w3o as a bad latency trade for a sub-ms same-user window. Revisit only if a perf budget or a CAS primitive appears. w3o's stamp->insert coupling already guarantees OUR own entry is consistent with what we wrote.

title: Scoped locate ignores a stray #np-projects outside CONTROL_DIR that full-scan would pick first (486 D2)
description: Follow-up from 486 plan review (2026-07-07). parse_project_control (parser/projects.rs) picks the FIRST #np-projects note by relative_path across the WHOLE store and does NOT filter is_excluded_relative. The scoped cold-load locates the control note by scanning only CONTROL_DIR (Notes/_NotePlan Organizer). If a SECOND #np-projects note exists outside CONTROL_DIR in a folder sorting before '_NotePlan Organizer' (e.g. Notes/AAA/…; '_'=0x5F > 'A'=0x41) and that folder isn't itself a resolved project, the full scan picks Notes/AAA/… while the scoped locate picks the _NotePlan Organizer one → different board. Pathological (app only ever creates one control note, in CONTROL_DIR) and non-destructive, self-corrects on Rescan. Align locate semantics (make parse_project_control prefer CONTROL_DIR / filter excluded, OR force full fallback when a stray tagged note is detected).
close_reason: Resolved in 486: parse_project_control + parse_backlog_control now prefer CONTROL_DIR via shared control_dir_sort_key, so a stray/archived #np-projects sorting earlier can no longer win in the full-store locate. Scoped and full locates provably converge. Tests: test_control_dir_note_wins_over_stray_sorting_earlier (both projects + backlog).

title: Scoped cold-load: ranked task outside any project folder/Calendar shows stale until Rescan (486 D1)
description: Follow-up from 486 plan review (2026-07-07). The scoped cold-load path (get_backlog before the first full scan warms the cache) scans only {control folder + resolved project folders + Calendar/}. A ranked #np-backlog entry whose target block-id lives on a task OUTSIDE any resolved project folder and outside Calendar/ resolves live under the full scan but shows resolved:false (stale) under the scoped cold path. UNREACHABLE via the app's own ranking flow (pool tasks only come from project folders/Calendar), but reachable by hand-editing #np-backlog to rank an arbitrary note's task. Non-destructive, read-only display, self-corrects after a manual Rescan (warm cache → full build). Documented as a known cold-path limitation in scan_scoped; file to track a proper mitigation (e.g. detect + fall back to full scan) if it ever bites.

title: Tombstone GC: compact accumulated blank/comment tombstone lines in #np-backlog
description: Follow-up from ih2 (2026-07-07). ih2 removes a ranked entry by edit_line-blanking it to a tombstone (empty or <!-- np-backlog: removed --> line) rather than delete_lines (which now needs a broken-dryRun confirmationToken). Repeated removals accumulate blank/comment tombstone lines in the app-owned #np-backlog control note — harmless (reader ENTRY_RE ignores them, reorder skips them) but untidy. Add opportunistic GC: when the app next rewrites the ranked block (e.g. on reorder), drop accumulated tombstone lines. App-owned note only; still verify-before-write.

title: Unify folder specificity metric: project_for_path uses byte-length, resolve_folder uses segment-count
description: Follow-up from 241/dz4 code-review (2026-07-07). Two divergent notions of folder depth/specificity now coexist: parser/projects.rs resolve_folder's folder_rank ranks by segment count (path.matches('/').count()) to pick the SHALLOWEST match; parser/backlog.rs project_for_path (~L155) ranks by folder.len() (byte length, max_by_key) to pick the DEEPEST (most specific) match for attributing a task to a nested project. Byte-length is a weaker proxy than segment count: a long single-segment folder name can outrank a short nested path, so project_for_path could mis-attribute a task to the wrong project when candidate folder string lengths don't track nesting depth. Extract a shared folder_depth(path)->usize helper (segment count) and use it in both places so specificity means segment-count consistently. Low priority; contrived to trigger today (fixture folders are JD-prefixed + uniquely nested).

title: resolve_folder: duplicate-JD-id refs resolve lexicographically — surface as a warning instead of silently picking
description: Follow-up from 241/dz4 plan review (2026-07-07). After the 241 fix (shallowest+lexicographic tiebreak), a JD-prefixed reference that matches >1 folder sharing the same leading JD id (itself a JD-consistency violation the app already flags) resolves deterministically but SILENTLY to the shallowest/lexicographically-first candidate. Strictly better than the prior order-dependence, but the ambiguity is hidden. Consider surfacing a ProjectControl warning when resolve_folder had multiple JD-id candidates, so the user learns their JD ids collide.

title: Backlog: surface ranked-only project groups under active search
description: Follow-up from mu1 plan review (2026-07-07). Backlog project groups are created only while iterating the search-filtered pool (Backlog.tsx:134,148), so a project with ranked tasks matching the query but ZERO open tasks matching renders no header at all under search — its ranked matches aren't surfaced. mu1's count fix makes displayed counts coherent for groups that DO render but doesn't change which groups render. Decide whether such ranked-only groups should surface a header under active search.

title: resolve_folder: exclude Calendar-kind notes from ancestor-folder matching
description: Pre-existing (found during PR #10 review, not introduced there): projects.rs resolve_folder walks every non-excluded note's ancestor directories matching against #np-projects references — Calendar notes are not excluded, so a project reference that textually resolves to 'Calendar' (contrived: a project literally named Calendar) would claim the Calendar folder as a project folder and mislabel calendar tasks with project metadata. Guard: skip notes whose kind maps to CalendarKind (or paths under Calendar/) in the ancestor walk. Add a regression test.
close_reason: Merged to main (PR #12-#16).

title: 'Show older daily tasks' unreachable when zero in-window calendar tasks exist
description: Final-review Minor from PR #10: the include-older toggle renders inside the Calendar inventory group, which only renders when calendarTasks.length > 0 || includeOlder — a vault whose only calendar tasks are >30 days old (or a search filtering them all out) can never reveal them. Render the group header + toggle whenever dailies were window-excluded (backend could report an excluded count, or frontend always shows the toggle when the Calendar group would otherwise be empty).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 (squash 63a7795): Board = ranked work queue, merged Backlog with grouped inventory + calendar harvesting; r2t metadata absorbed into TaskCard; b68 Show-older reachability fixed via Copilot followup

title: Backlog.tsx polish: request cancellation on load; hoist duplicated matchesQuery
description: Final-review Minors from PR #10: (1) Backlog's load() has no cancelled guard (Board.tsx has one) — rapid basePath switches can apply a stale vault's response; mirror Board's cancelled-flag pattern or key the component. (2) matchesQuery search-filter logic is duplicated inside two useMemos (eslint exhaustive-deps workaround) — hoist to a module-level pure function taking (query, text, tags). Also from review: group-header counts mix filtered (open) and unfiltered (ranked) numbers while searching — label or filter consistently.
notes: 2026-07-06: matchesQuery duplication RESOLVED in PR #10's code-review pass (shared matchesSearch in utils/taskMeta.ts). Remaining scope: load() request-cancellation guard (mirror Board's cancelled flag) and the filtered-open vs unfiltered-ranked group-header count mismatch under search.
close_reason: Merged to main (PR #12-#16).

title: Make NoteKind calendar-mapping match arms exhaustive (two _ => None sites)
description: Final-review note from PR #10: parser/period.rs calendar_period and models/backlog.rs CalendarKind::from_note_kind both use '_ => None' wildcards — a future NoteKind variant silently classifies as non-calendar instead of forcing a decision at the match site. Replace both with explicit 'NoteKind::Regular | NoteKind::Template => None' so adding a variant becomes a compile error. 2-line change + no behavior difference today.
close_reason: Merged to main (PR #12-#16).

title: Sprint-style planning: time-boxed chunks, capacity, scheduling onto daily notes / Apple Calendar
description: User vision 2026-07-04 (next round after the i0g Priorities IA redesign): plan ranked tasks in sprint-like time chunks (e.g. 2 weeks, user-configurable) to build a sense of capacity — 'look at my work + personal schedule, figure out how much time I have, then plan when I'll actually do them.' Desired outcomes: (a) chunked planning view over the ranked work queue; (b) ideally schedule tasks onto NotePlan DAILY NOTES with times (matching how the user plans days today) — write path via MCP, all data-safety guards apply; (c) Apple Calendar (EventKit) as an acceptable alternative target — note the MCP server exposes an eventkit tool (action get_events, requires source; per 8zl its schema needs Inspector verification before use). Big feature: own brainstorm/spec cycle when picked up; depends on i0g landing first.

title: NotePlan Spaces support
description: Use noteplan_folders.list_spaces to support multiple vaults.

title: Calendar cross-reference analyzer
description: Use noteplan_eventkit to find days with calendar events but no daily notes.
